bead.title: spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource spec bead was blocked because the governing docs named cleanup scope in broad terms but did not give implementers exact policy values for soft resource pressure, minimum scratch age, and recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes. Without those choices, cleanup implementation beads can either under-clean leaked DDx roots or overreach into non-DDx temp directories.

CURRENT STATE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:585-650 defines execution resource cleanup, but lines 605-608 still say `soft high-water threshold` and `hard floor` without concrete values.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:618-623 includes known DDx prefixes and minimum age/mtime rules, but it does not list the prefixes or the default age.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:224-229 and 277-288 repeat the same policy without values.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:13-16 currently defines only a last-resort hard floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:16-18 defines `cleanup.json` as the ownership metadata file, and cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:212-220 currently scans only entries whose names start with `ExecuteBeadWtPrefix`.

PROPOSED FIX
Update FEAT-010, SD-025, and P8 with explicit policy constants and anchors:
- Recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes: `ddx-exec-wt`, `ddx-claim-heartbeats`, `ddx-metric-keepalive`, `ddx-test-`, `ddx-e2e-`, and any temp root containing DDx `cleanup.json` metadata with matching project ownership.
- Minimum age: metadata-backed paths may be removed when liveness is expired or the owning attempt is terminal; metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours and no live PID/session evidence.
- Hard floor: preserve the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB free bytes and 1024 free inodes until a config override bead changes it.
- Soft high-water cleanup trigger: run cleanup before claim when any checked temp/evidence root is below 512 MiB free bytes or below 8192 free inodes, then re-check before claiming.
- Insertion anchors: FEAT-010 `### Execution Resource Cleanup` after lines 600-608 and 618-623; SD-025 `### Roots and ownership` lines 224-229 and `### Resource preflight and loop-fatal handling` lines 285-288; reliability P8 lines 47-56.
- Remove the `needs_human` blocker once those explicit policy choices are documented.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement cleanup code changes in this spec bead.
- Do not permit deletion of arbitrary non-DDx temp directories.
- Do not change provider quota, routing, review policy, or candidate-cycle behavior.
- Do not delete complete `.ddx/runs/<id>`, complete `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>`, preserved worktrees, active registered worktrees, or `refs/ddx/iterations/...`.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-f3dfde0d. No deps.
bead.acceptance:
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md explicitly lists the DDx-owned scratch prefixes and `cleanup.json` metadata rule named in this bead.
2. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the default minimum age rule: expired/terminal metadata-backed paths may be removed, and metadata-less recognized-prefix scratch roots require mtime age >= 6 hours plus no live PID/session evidence.
3. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the soft cleanup trigger as below 512 MiB free bytes or below 8192 free inodes before claim, and preserves the current hard stop floor of 64 MiB and 1024 inodes.
4. docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md mirrors the same prefix, age, soft-trigger, and hard-floor values in its roots/ownership and resource-preflight sections.
5. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 references the explicit FEAT-010/SD-025 cleanup policy so future worker beads do not treat leaked DDx test/e2e scratch roots as out of scope.
6. The bead no longer carries a `needs_human` label after those values are recorded.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

{"rationale":"Bead is well-formed against all 8 rubric criteria. Title is imperative and names subsystem + change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE (execution_cleanup.go:202-212, execution_resources.go:13-16, execute_bead_loop.go:501-503/550-551, reliability-principles.md:47-54), PROPOSED FIX, and NON-SCOPE. AC items are numbered, AC 4 names three concrete test identifiers (TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs, TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor, TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup), and AC 5 includes the lefthook gate. Labels carry phase, area, kind, and two spec cross-refs (spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025). Parent and deps are explicit. Description is self-contained with no /tmp refs or chat-context dependencies. The sole rubric gap — absence of an explicit `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpaths\u003e/... green` AC line — is waived because NON-SCOPE explicitly prohibits code changes; lefthook pre-commit subsumes the test gate for a docs-only bead. Repeated no-changes execution failures are attributable to routing degradation (resolved_provider empty across attempts) and harness unavailability, not bead quality.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":["go-test-gate: docs-only spec bead (NON-SCOPE prohibits code changes); lefthook pre-commit subsumes the test-pass gate"]}

{"detail":"Three consecutive no_changes exits indicate the agent cannot determine what concrete text to write. Three specific gaps make a safe rewrite impossible without inventing product semantics: (1) The soft high-water threshold and minimum mtime age are product-semantic choices — the spec is supposed to define these values but the bead does not supply them, so the agent cannot write spec text without guessing. (2) The DDx-owned scratch prefixes beyond ExecuteBeadWtPrefix are not named; the agent must research cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go to identify them, which is out of scope for a spec-writing task and produces non-deterministic output across runs. (3) The current state of FEAT-010 and SD-025 is not described — without knowing which sections exist, the agent has no anchor for where to insert the new content, which explains the repeated no-op exits. A safe rewrite would require the operator to supply: (a) the specific additional DDx-owned temp prefixes found in the codebase, (b) the chosen soft high-water value in MiB/%, and (c) the chosen minimum scratch-dir age threshold in minutes. Without those, any rewrite risks widening scope (scanning all of /tmp) or hardcoding policy values the bead author has not approved.","intake_outcome":"ambiguous_needs_human"}

{"rationale":"Strong spec bead with good problem/root-cause/fix/non-scope structure. Deductions: (1) AC item 7 is the lefthook gate but there is no 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpaths\u003e/... green' line — this is a docs-only bead so no Go test filter exists, which is acceptable, but the rubric expects acknowledgment; (2) AC items reference exact doc sections only loosely — no line numbers in the AC itself (description has them, AC does not); (3) description file:line anchors are present but some are approximate ranges rather than pinpoint lines (e.g. 585-650 is a wide span); (4) no cross-ref labels to prevention or ADR artifacts despite touching reliability-principles P8; (5) 'needs_human' label is referenced in description/AC but not present in the labels array — the bead implies removing it but doesn't show it as currently set, creating ambiguity for the agent.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Add a note in AC or description confirming no Go compilation unit changes are involved and therefore no go test filter applies (satisfies rubric criterion 4 for doc-only beads).","Tighten AC items 1-5 to include the specific line ranges from the description (e.g. 'after line 608 in FEAT-010') so the agent has a precise insertion target without re-reading the description.","Add a cross-ref label such as 'adr:cleanup-policy' or 'prevention:resource-exhaustion' if a governing ADR or prevention doc exists, to satisfy rubric criterion 6.","Either add 'needs_human' to the labels array (showing current state) or add an explicit AC item 'needs_human label is removed from this bead on completion' so the agent knows it must mutate the bead metadata as part of the work.","Narrow the description file:line spans where possible (e.g. 585-650 could be split into the specific sub-sections that need editing) to reduce agent search time."],"waivers_applied":["No go test filter required: bead touches only .md documentation files; Go compilation is unaffected. Rubric criterion 4 (named Test* function) is waived for doc-only beads."]}

{"rationale":"Criteria checked against the 8-criterion rubric:
1. TITLE ✓ — imperative, names subsystem and change.
2. ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE ✓ — CURRENT STATE section provides multiple specific file:line anchors across docs and Go source.
3. NUMBERED AC, EACH VERIFIABLE ✓ — 7 numbered items, each checkable against a named file or observable state.
4. AT LEAST ONE AC NAMES A Test* FUNCTION OR go test -run FILTER ✗ — No Test* function or go test -run filter appears in any AC item. This is a docs-only spec bead (kind:spec, area:docs) so a waiver is defensible, but the rubric requires explicit naming or an explicit waiver label.
5. FINAL TWO AC LINES: `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` THEN `lefthook run pre-commit passes` ✗ — The final two AC items are (6) the needs_human label removal and (7) lefthook. The required `cd cli && go test` penultimate line is absent entirely.
6. LABELS: phase:N + area:* + kind:* + cross-refs ✓ — phase:2, area:docs, area:agent, kind:spec, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025 all present.
7. PARENT + DEPS EXPLICIT ✓ — Parent ddx-f3dfde0d named; 'No deps' stated in description.
8. STANDALONE DESCRIPTION ✓ — No /tmp references, no chat-context dependencies; description is self-contained.","score":6,"suggested_fixes":["Add `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green` as AC item 7 (shifting the current items 6 and 7 to 7 and 8). Even for a docs-only bead the gate is required by convention; it also catches any accidental Go file edits that break compilation.","Either name a related test function in at least one AC item (e.g., 'TestExecutionCleanup* still passes after doc-only change confirms no Go source was altered') or add a waiver label such as `waiver:no-test-function` and record the rationale ('docs-only spec bead; no Go code changed') so the drain loop does not flag criterion 4 as a hard failure."],"waivers_applied":[]}
